title: System Monitoring Remediations
what_are_these_remediations: System Monitoring Remediations are remedial measures targeting the underlying monitoring systems which operate when the AI/LLM System is running.
why_remediate: System Monitoring processes are the first layer of defense in regard to mitigating threats.
Many threats within this category are low level threats and prevented by controls on the system running the AI/LLM itself.
how_to_remediate: The majority of the threats that can be mitigated by this category are generally rectified by placing a WAF or host-level control which analyzes the contents of data travelling in or out of a given network infrastructure.
The utilization of a logging system generally facilitates remediation for threats relevant to this category as well.
remediation_difficulty: Low — mitigation efforts are generally implementations to existing systems with already existing solutions, providing a benefit to engineers.

associated_mitigations.name: Control Access to AI Models and Data in Production

title: Model Hardening Remediations
what_are_these_remediations: Model Hardening Remediations consist of efforts which intend to improve model outputs by adjusting the internal model configurations, modelfile level parameters, or request schema of a given model used.
why_remediate: This is a Defense in Depth solution.
This remediation effort improves the general output for your model pertinent to its use case.
how_to_remediate: These efforts revolve around editing the native configurations that are possible with any given model.
We can adjust internal parameters for open source models such as top_k, temperature, grammar roots, and internal system prompt.
remediation_difficulty: Low to Medium — time spent can range from as little as editing a modelfile or API request structure, to as much as generating or distilling a completely new model. Testing must occur to rectify issues within this category.

title: Input Sanitization Remediations
what_are_these_remediations: Input Sanitization remediation efforts are those which occur for threats that exist against input receiving processes on a given AI/LLM powered system. These efforts consist of actions taken to prevent the abuse of a model from malicious requests intending to gain extraneous access to possible capabilities regarding your system.
why_remediate: These mitigation methods serve to prevent the progression of an attacker through attack chain stages, generally to a degree which prevents known flaws from becoming vulnerabilities.
how_to_remediate: These mitigation methods are easy to implement, are low in effort to plan, and prevent significant corruption and/or destruction of infrastructure associated with your assets.
If a model itself is found to permit an excessive amount of queries to an endpoint, implement a rate limit on the system.
Sanitize training data that is given to the model in pre-production.
Utilize input restoration to transform all inputs into safe versions of inputs for your AI/LLM powered machine.
Implement Proactive and Reactive Logging solutions monitoring endpoints which receive input.
remediation_difficulty: Medium — time associated with remediations in this fault localization category are noticeable but not significant. Expect remediation timeframes similar to development timeframes for the affected process at hand.

associated_mitigations.name: Restrict Number of AI Model Queries
Input and Output Validation for AI Agent Components

title: Process Sanitization Remediations
what_are_these_remediations: Process Sanitization is the act of sanitizing the data which is processed by a given AI/LLM System.
why_remediate: For any possible processing that can occur on any given AI/LLM System, there should be a thorough understanding of exactly what is intended to occur and what is not intended to occur.

These mitigation efforts primarily rectify the issues which occur from attacks existing in the middle of the attack chain, implying a higher severity for threat IDs triggering mitigations for this category.
how_to_remediate: If you permit the model access to only certain directories through allow/disallow lists, then the model can only traverse those directories regardless of what the user asks.
Sanitize the tools by which your model uses, for given contexts, by only allowing certain tools to be used by the model.
If an AI system utilizes an array of commands, its processes should be sanitized.
If an AI system utilizes a singular tool, that process should be sanitized.
If an AI system can read internal machine contents, that process itself should be sanitized.
remediation_difficulty: Medium — time associated with remediations in this fault localization category are noticeable but not significant. Expect remediation timeframes similar to development timeframes for the affected process at hand.

associated_mitigations.name: Restrict AI Agent Tool Invocation on Untrusted Data
Segmentation of AI Agents and Components

title: Output Sanitization Remediations
what_are_these_remediations: Output sanitization is the action of remediating processes associated with data output on a given AI/LLM System.
why_remediate: Output created by a model triggered by a user is output which is created and presented by an autonomous machine — in the context of security, it is not inherently safe or inert.

Most forms of compromise that require output sanitization efforts imply that Access Gain is possible; the tactics utilized here generally have progressed to a state in which a prompt creates a malicious output.
how_to_remediate: The execution of code, malicious or not, can be avoided by transmuting the content into a data string that cannot be interpreted by any machine.
Disallow the rendering of output referring to sensitive directories during reasoning or thinking stages of interactions.
Require that output tokens from the AI/LLM Model are converted to an inert state before being presented on any application and before being transported across a network, internal or external.
remediation_difficulty: Medium — time associated with remediations in this fault localization category are noticeable but not significant. Expect remediation timeframes similar to development timeframes for the affected process at hand.

associated_mitigations.name: Input and Output Validation for AI Agent Components
